subject: Re: GUI crashing without error when using neural networks
body:
Hello Michael,

I've been having the same problem on a windows 10. The only solution has 
been to bin. In case your thinking about installing a new graphics card, we 
installed a new gpu (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 TI) with more memory and still 
get the same spike and crash.  I think you're onto something with the build 
thing so please let me know how the windows 7 install turned out. I would 
also appreciate it if anyone had any clues and/or alternative solution to 
this.

Good luck Michael and thanks in advance to anyone with clues,
Dominique Gutierrez

On Friday, February 8, 2019 at 11:19:25 AM UTC-5, Michael Saur wrote:
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> We recently installed Warp on a Windows 10 machine to test it. It seems to 
> run fine for CTF estimation and motion correction. However, whenever I want 
> to use any neural network-based calculation, i.e. picking and denoising, 
> the GPU activity in the Task Manager briefly spikes to 100% and the Warp 
> GUI crashes. The LastCrash.txt file does not show any error.
>
> The Event Viewer log states the following:
>
> Faulting application name: Warp.exe, version: 1.0.6.0, time stamp: 
> 0x5bf461d6
>
> Faulting module name: ucrtbase.dll, version: 10.0.17763.1, time stamp: 
> 0x309241e0
>
> Exception code: 0xc0000409
>
> Fault offset: 0x000000000006f08e
>
> Faulting process id: 0xce4
>
> Faulting application start time: 0x01d4bf1122b93f85
>
> Faulting application path: C:\Warp\Warp.exe
>
> Faulting module path: C:\windows\System32\ucrtbase.dll
>
> Report Id: 040a30f6-fb31-4ba0-86dd-b8bc859cdacd
>
> Faulting package full name: 
>
> Faulting package-relative application ID:
>
>
> Has anybody encountered this specific behaviour and perhaps solved it? We 
> wanted to try a different build or a Windows 7 install next, to rule out 
> build-specific issues.
>
>
> Thanks in advance for any clues,
>
>
> Michael
>
>
